symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive asymmetric distal limb weakness with acute worsening and near-falls

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive LMN-pattern limb weakness is core to ALS; acute worsening signals decompensation and falls risk.
A weeks-long progressive course is inconsistent with acute stroke.

symptoms.symptom_name: Choking episode on saliva with prolonged coughing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Choking on secretions indicates failed airway protection — high aspiration risk.
An aspiration event is the precursor to aspiration pneumonitis/pneumonia.

symptoms.symptom_name: New visible muscle fasciculations in arms (and tongue, carer-observed)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fasciculation reflects LMN degeneration; widespread fasciculation supports ALS over focal mimics.
A wet voice indicates secretions on the vocal cords and bulbar weakness — a marker of aspiration risk.
Impaired secretion clearance drives the choking and aspiration risk and is the target of drying agents.

symptoms.symptom_name: Shallow breathing and breathlessness at rest with anxiety about respiratory muscles

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Respiratory-muscle weakness is the leading cause of death in ALS; new resting breathlessness is a critical warning.

symptoms.symptom_name: SpO2 94% on home fingertip oximeter at rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Borderline resting hypoxia in a neuromuscular patient is concerning; patient-obtainable and admissible in telehealth but unverified.
Tachypnoea can reflect early respiratory compromise or aspiration; carer-observed so admissible but imprecise.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mixed UMN/LMN signs — lower-limb hyperreflexia with right extensor plantar (Babinski), upper-limb hyporeflexia and flaccid weakness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Concurrent UMN and LMN signs across regions is the hallmark of ALS — sealed clinician exam finding, not elicitable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound muscle atrophy of hands and intrinsic foot muscles; tongue fasciculation on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Focal wasting and tongue fasciculation confirm LMN degeneration — clinician exam finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Shallow diaphragmatic excursion with clear lung fields on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced diaphragmatic excursion with clear lungs points to ventilatory-muscle weakness rather than parenchymal disease — sealed exam finding.
Sphincter sparing is typical of ALS and helps argue against a cord/cauda pathology.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever and no purulent sputum at presentation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/purulent sputum makes established pneumonia less likely NOW, but does not exclude an evolving one after the aspiration event.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Bulbar failure / aspiration risk in ALS
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Choking on saliva plus a wet voice plus secretion pooling together mark failed airway protection and a high aspiration risk requiring urgent swallowing precautions and review.
Resting breathlessness, borderline hypoxia, tachypnoea and shallow diaphragmatic excursion together signal falling ventilatory reserve — the leading cause of death in ALS.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person clinical assessment (airway, breathing, aspiration screen)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Airway, respiratory and aspiration status cannot be examined via chat; same-day in-person evaluation is the pivotal step.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Chest X-ray if aspiration pneumonia suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Low threshold for imaging given the aspiration event with tachypnoea/hypoxia, especially if fever or productive cough develop.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Respiratory function assessment (FVC / sniff nasal inspiratory pressure)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Objectifies respiratory-muscle reserve and guides NIV decisions in ALS.
Defines safe consistencies and the need for alternative feeding to reduce aspiration.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Lumbar puncture / MRI brain for acute stroke workup
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The temporal course and fasciculations make acute stroke very unlikely; extensive stroke imaging is over-investigation and delays the real priorities.
